notes: Layer 2 behavioural acceptance for the 10-PR batch merged to dev/dave at 2c34f4ed6 on 2026-07-20. Run against https://dev.getalloro.com. Layer 1 (tsc, 2050 backend + 223 frontend tests, check:conventions, pointer gate) already passed on the merged commit — these items prove behaviour the automated suite cannot reach. Items marked surface=ci run against the repo, not the app.

items.title: #177 — proof-receipt rejects an unauthenticated caller
items.precondition: None. dev deployed at or after 2c34f4ed6.
steps: 1. curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://dev.getalloro.com/api/proof-receipt
items.expected: 401. Confirms the route is mounted and behind auth — a 404 would mean it never deployed.
items.evidence: curl /api/proof-receipt (unauth) -> HTTP 401; also 401 with ?organization_id=1. Route mounted + auth-gated (not 404). 2026-07-21, dev live.
items.notes: Already observed 401 immediately post-deploy, but re-confirm on a clean run.

items.title: #177 — an owner reads their OWN org's receipt
items.precondition: A valid JWT for a user in org 39 (One Endodontics).
steps: 1. GET /api/proof-receipt?organization_id=39&locationId=<an accessible location> with the org-39 JWT
2. Inspect the body
items.expected: 200, and the body is exactly { success: true, data: {...}, error: null } (§8.1). Items are location-tagged.
items.notes: BLOCKED — needs a valid org-39 JWT (auth). Not runnable from Claude Code without credentials.

items.title: #177 — TENANT ISOLATION: org A cannot read org B's receipt
steps: 1. GET /api/proof-receipt?organization_id=5 with the org-39 JWT
2. Repeat for two or three other org ids
items.expected: 403 every time, with no data body. This is the defect the PR existed to close — before the fix it returned 200 with the other org's data.
items.notes: BLOCKED — needs a valid org-39 JWT. This is the security-critical tenant-isolation check (org A must 403 on org B's receipt). Run this one first once auth is available.

items.title: #177 — a location outside the caller's scope is refused
items.precondition: A JWT for a user scoped to one location of a multi-location org.
steps: 1. GET /api/proof-receipt?organization_id=<own org>&locationId=<a location in the same org the user cannot access>
items.expected: 403. The param is camelCase locationId — the scope middleware only reads that spelling, and the controller independently re-checks it against accessibleLocationIds.
items.notes: BLOCKED — needs a JWT scoped to one location of a multi-location org.

items.title: #183 — Get Found impressions renders without contradicting its own card
items.precondition: Signed in to dev as a user with a GSC-connected org (org 39).
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard and find the Patient Journey / Get Found stage
2. Note the impressions figure
3. Open the detail deck for that stage
4. Read the impressions, Google clicks and CTR shown together
items.expected: clicks ÷ impressions equals the displayed CTR, and every metric's label states the source it actually comes from. No metric is labelled 'Search Console + Business Profile' while showing a Search-only number.
items.evidence: Pilot into org 39 (One Endodontics), Patient Journey Insights. Pipeline: 23,798 Google Visibility -> 6% -> 1,432 Website Visitors -> 2% -> 25 Website Leads. Math consistent: 1432/23798=6.02% (shown 6%); 25/1432=1.75% (shown 2% in arrow, 1.7% in callout — both correct roundings). Source labels honest: 'How often you showed up on Google' / 'Rybbit website visitors' / 'Verified submissions'. No metric mislabeled. 2026-07-21.
items.notes: Cosmetic nit (not a failure): same 25/1432 ratio renders as 2% (arrow) and 1.7% (callout) — different rounding precision, both truthful.

items.title: #183 — Maps term is zero, and the gauge says so honestly
items.precondition: psql access to the dev database, read-only.
steps: 1. SET default_transaction_read_only = on;
2. SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE (gbp_data->'yesterday'->'visibility'->>'impressions_maps_desktop')::int > 0) AS nonzero, count(*) AS total FROM google_data_store WHERE run_type='daily';
3. Compare against what the Get Found stage displays
items.expected: If nonzero = 0, the displayed impressions equal the organic-only figure and the UI makes no claim to include Maps. The number must not move purely because the Maps plumbing landed.
items.notes: BLOCKED — needs read-only psql access to the dev DB. Dave's side. THIS IS THE ZERO-MAPS GATE: until answered, the Get-Found impressions number cannot move.

items.title: #183 — the nightly agent skips unmapped locations instead of writing zeros
items.precondition: Read-only DB access. Orgs 42/44/46/47 have locations with no google_properties row.
steps: 1. Note the current max(created_at) in the Proofline insight table for one of those locations
2. Wait for, or trigger, the next daily agent run
3. Re-query
items.expected: No new insight row is written for an unmapped location, and no Claude call is spent on it. Pre-fix, it ran on an empty payload and could persist an insight built entirely on zeros.
items.notes: BLOCKED — needs read-only dev DB access (orgs 42/44/46/47 unmapped locations). Dave's side.

items.title: #175 — the rank card never shows a cross-universe fraction
items.precondition: Signed in to dev; an org with a completed practice ranking.
steps: 1. Open the Patient Journey context cards
2. Read the ranking card
items.expected: No '#N of M' fraction anywhere. The denominator was removed from the contract entirely, so the shape is unrepresentable — not merely hidden.
items.notes: RUN 2026-07-21, org 39 pilot: client rank card shows 'YOU RANK #1 of 11 nearby' + 'You lead all 10 tracked competitors' (11 = you + 10 mapped competitors — self-consistent, NOT the impossible '#15 of 5' cross-universe bug #175 fixed). AMBIGUOUS vs T8's literal wording ('no #N of M fraction anywhere; denominator removed from contract entirely'). '#1 of 11' is textually an N-of-M. NEEDS DAVE'S RULING — he owns #175 and the acceptance wording. CC read: intended map-pack count, not the bug. Not stamped pass or fail.

items.title: #175 — a practice outside the local top 20 gets an honest empty state
items.precondition: An org whose latest ranking has search_status indicating no top-20 match.
steps: 1. Open the ranking card for that org
items.expected: Reads 'Not in the local top 20 yet' — not a zero, not a blank, and not 'Run a ranking' for a practice that already ran one.
items.notes: CANNOT VERIFY on org 39 — One Endodontics ranks #1, so the 'Not in the local top 20 yet' empty state cannot render here. Needs a DIFFERENT org whose latest ranking has no top-20 match. 2026-07-21.

items.title: #182 — the public audit endpoint is rate limited per IP
items.precondition: None. This is an unauthenticated public endpoint.
steps: 1. POST an empty body to https://dev.getalloro.com/api/audit/start 11 times in under an hour from one IP
2. Record the status code and RateLimit-Remaining header on each
items.expected: The first 10 are accepted (400 on an empty body is fine — the limiter counts before the pipeline spends), the 11th returns 429.
items.evidence: 12x POST /api/audit/start, empty body {}, single IP: requests 1-10 -> HTTP 400 (limiter counts before pipeline spends), 11th & 12th -> HTTP 429. Per-IP cap trips on the 11th. 2026-07-21, dev live.
items.notes: Use an EMPTY body so the limiter counts without triggering the paid Apify/Places/LLM pipeline (~$0.10-0.30 per real run).

items.title: #182 — the limiter keys on the real client IP, not the proxy
items.precondition: Two distinct source IPs (e.g. laptop and a phone on cellular).
steps: 1. Exhaust the limit from IP A per T10
2. Immediately POST once from IP B
items.expected: IP B succeeds. If it is also 429, trust proxy is misconfigured and the limiter is a GLOBAL cap on the public audit funnel — a lead-gen outage, not a protection.
items.notes: PARTIAL — T10 already confirmed the IP-A half (-> 429). Full check needs a 2nd distinct source IP (proves the limiter keys on real client IP, not a global cap).

items.title: #178 — generated audits no longer claim capabilities Alloro lacks
items.precondition: Ability to run an audit on dev and read the generated output.
steps: 1. Run an audit against a practice whose GBP has few photos and few reviews (maximising the chance the model reaches for those levers)
2. Read every recommendation, including the GBP and Visual Authority sections
items.expected: No recommendation offers Alloro review generation, GBP photo refresh, or a live PMS connection. Review REPLY automation and PMS upload/mapping may still be offered — those are real.
items.notes: BLOCKED — needs to run an audit on dev and read the output. Awaiting Corey sign-in / audit run.

items.title: #189 — the spec gate blocks a self-inconsistent spec
items.precondition: A local checkout of dev/dave at or after 2c34f4ed6.
steps: 1. bash scripts/check-spec-parity.sh --self-test
2. Edit any plans/*/spec.html so the hero pill and the status card disagree
3. Re-run the gate against that file
items.expected: Self-test passes all fixtures. The doctored spec exits non-zero and NAMES both conflicting values. An unparseable spec exits non-zero rather than reporting 'nothing to assert'.
items.evidence: bash scripts/check-spec-parity.sh --self-test -> 22 passed / 0 failed / 22 cases, exit 0. Doctored specs (pill-completed-card-pending, class-swap-pending-to-completed, etc.) exit 1; no-status-surface + empty-pill exit 3. Ran on detached worktree at dev/dave 66f1bf7af.
items.notes: The gate is still continue-on-error in CI, so it reports but cannot fail a run. Promotion to required is a separate decision.

items.title: #181 — the ledger tool is non-destructive and cannot deadlock CI
steps: 1. Record the sha256 and mtime of PR-LOG.md
2. Run: bash scripts/pr-log.sh -check   (a deliberate typo)
3. Re-check sha256 and mtime
4. Confirm no workflow triggers pr-log.sh on pull_request
items.expected: The typo exits 2 and PR-LOG.md is byte-identical with an unchanged mtime. grep -r 'pr-log' .github/workflows/ shows only the scheduled/dispatch refresh workflow — never a pull_request trigger.
items.evidence: bash scripts/pr-log.sh --<bad-flag> -> exit 2; PR-LOG.md byte-identical (sha256 + mtime unchanged); bad --limit -> exit 2 (source: line 116 *) exit 2, line 121 bad-limit exit 2). grep -rl pr-log .github/workflows -> only pr-log-refresh.yml (on: schedule cron '0 6 * * *' + workflow_dispatch; comment: NEVER on pull_request). dev/dave 66f1bf7af.
items.notes: Pre-fix, any unrecognised argument silently overwrote the file and exited 0.

items.title: #174 — PR CI actually fails on a bad PR
items.precondition: Ability to open a throwaway PR against dev/dave.
steps: 1. Branch from dev/dave, introduce a deliberate TypeScript error, open a PR
2. Watch the PR Checks run
3. Close the PR and delete the branch
items.expected: The run goes RED on the backend typecheck step. A gate never observed failing is not a gate — this is the only item that proves the whole batch's safety net works.
items.notes: Dave's side per the protocol adoption checklist. A new throwaway PR — Rule 4 holds (no new branches from Claude Code until this batch is validated).
